posts.title: 5 GST Tips Every Small Business Owner Must Know
posts.excerpt: GST compliance can be tricky for small businesses. Here are 5 simple tips to stay compliant, avoid penalties, and make the most of Input Tax Credit.
posts.content:
## 5 GST Tips Every Small Business Owner Must Know

Running a small business comes with many responsibilities — and GST compliance is one of the most important ones.

### 1. File Returns on Time
Always file GSTR-1 and GSTR-3B before the due date. Late filing attracts penalty of ₹50 per day (₹20 for nil returns).

### 2. Reconcile ITC Every Month
Match your Input Tax Credit in GSTR-2B before claiming it in GSTR-3B. Wrong ITC claims can attract notices.

### 3. Maintain Proper Invoices
Every B2B sale must have a proper GST invoice with GSTIN, HSN code, and tax breakup.

### 4. Track Your Turnover
If your turnover crosses ₹40 lakhs (goods) or ₹20 lakhs (services), GST registration is mandatory.

### 5. Consult a CA Regularly
Tax laws change frequently. A qualified CA keeps your business updated and compliant at all times.

---

posts.title: Key GST Changes You Must Know in 2025
posts.excerpt: The GST Council has introduced several important changes that every business owner must be aware of. Here's a quick summary of what's new and how it affects you.
posts.content:
## Key GST Changes in 2025

The GST Council has introduced several important changes that every business owner must be aware of.

### 1. E-Invoicing Threshold Reduced
Businesses with turnover above ₹5 crore are now required to generate e-invoices for all B2B transactions.

### 2. ITC Reconciliation Made Stricter
Input Tax Credit claims must now match 100% with GSTR-2B before filing GSTR-3B.

### 3. Annual Return Simplified
GSTR-9 has been simplified for small taxpayers with turnover below ₹2 crore.

## What Should You Do?

- Review your e-invoicing setup immediately
- Reconcile ITC every month before filing
- Consult your CA for any pending compliance
